title: Trademark Objection Show Cause Hearing Service in Kerala- BookMyTM - Trademark & ISO Services in Kerala

blocks.text: Expert representation for the final stage of trademark acceptance.
Fill the details below for a callback.
What is Trademark Objection Show Cause Hearing?
Trademark Objection Show Cause hearing is the final stage for acceptance or refusal of the Trademark application in India.
Generally, the hearing date is informed via mail or physical copy. However, it is advisable to keep watching the trademark status every 10 days once the status shows ‘Ready for Show Cause Hearing’.
Do not leave the hearing unattended. Failure to appear often results in the refusal of the Trademark Application without considering its merit.
Fill the details below for a callback.
Normally, an applicant is awarded 3 hearings to defend objections. Extensions can be filed (Form TM-M) if needed.
Hearings take place at Chennai, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Delhi, and Kolkata registries.
Duly signed to authorize our attorney.
Invoices, Bills, Ads, P&L Statements proving usage.
Select package, fill form & make payment.
Detailed legal arguments often cannot be conveyed in a written reply. The hearing is your chance to speak to the Registrar directly.
Since 2020, hearings are conducted via Video Conferencing. You can sit in our office in Kochi while our attorney presents your case to the Hearing Officer in Chennai or Mumbai.
Often, the Hearing Officer decides on the spot. If convinced, they will move the application to "Accepted & Advertised." If not, they may ask for more written submissions.
Sometimes, you need to buy time (e.g., to gather 10-year-old invoices).
You can request an adjournment, but the Registry is strict. You generally get a maximum of 2 adjournments.
You must file the request at least 3 days before the hearing. Ignoring a hearing notice leads to immediate "Abandonment" of your application. Reviving it after that is difficult and costly.

items.q: 1. What is Trademark Objection Show Cause Hearing?
items.a: It is the final stage for acceptance or refusal of a Trademark. The hearing date is issued by the Registry, and an attorney must appear to defend the application.

items.q: 2. Is it necessary to appear for the hearing?
items.a: Yes. Failing to appear will lead to the 'Refusal' of the Trademark application.

items.q: 3. What if the cause presented is not satisfactory?
items.a: If the hearing officer is not satisfied, the application will be refused. You can then challenge the order by approaching the High Court.
